_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Cross-Project Isolation Test Matrix

steps.description: Implement the cross-project isolation test matrix: (1) Add a second_project fixture to tests/integration/conftest.py — creates a second Project row alongside test_project, seeds both with work items, batches, docs, code-index rows, and job-like rows with distinct identifiers. (2) Create tests/integration/test_cross_project_isolation.py — a parametrized matrix asserting four axes: dashboard-route isolation (TestClient + app.dependency_overrides[get_db], project B response body never contains project A identifiers), iw-command isolation (read commands: project A identifiers absent from the project-B command output; mutating commands: project A rows byte-for-byte unchanged — never a vacuous 'rows unchanged after a read'), global-aggregation positive assertion (/docs and /jobs show both projects' data), and per-worktree-DB vs orch-DB boundary (exercises orch/config.py get_db_url/get_orch_db_url resolution and the _prefer fallback against two distinct testcontainers — not two unrelated sessions; referencing test_per_worktree_isolation.py and F-00062 rules). (3) KNOWN_LEAK allowlist keyed by route/command — each genuine leak entry carries a TODO(file-incident) placeholder + rationale and is xfail-ed; each placeholder is listed under 'Operator follow-up' in the step report; do NOT run /iw-new-incident from inside the worktree; no production code may be edited. (4) Makefile target test-isolation. (5) Update docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md, skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md (+ iw sync-skills --force), and ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md (item 3.4 DONE + changelog). Demonstrate each new test can fail by inverting one assertion inside the test file (never editing production code), recording failing output as tdd_red_evidence, then reverting the test-file edit. Confirm git diff origin/main -- orch/ dashboard/ executor/ scripts/ is empty before reporting completion. Test-only CR — no production code may be edited. 2400s timeout.

Per-agent review of S01: scope discipline (no production code touched; git diff origin/main -- orch/ dashboard/ executor/ scripts/ is empty; no ai-dev/active/I-NNNNN/** created in the worktree), AC1 second_project fixture correctness (both projects seeded with full entity set, no ID overlap), AC2 dashboard-route isolation matrix (get_db override required, project A identifiers absent from project B response, parametrized per route, KNOWN_LEAK entries carry TODO(file-incident) placeholders + rationale + xfail + listed under 'Operator follow-up' in S01 report — a real Incident ID in KNOWN_LEAK is CRITICAL), AC3 iw-command isolation assertions (read commands assert output isolation, mutating commands assert byte-for-byte row isolation, no vacuous read assertions), AC4 global-aggregation positive assertion (both projects visible in /docs and /jobs), AC5 per-worktree-DB boundary check (exercises orch/config.py get_db_url/get_orch_db_url resolution + _prefer fallback, not two unrelated sessions), AC6 KNOWN_LEAK mechanism + tdd_red_evidence records deliberate-break demonstration (assertion inverted inside test file, confirmed RED, reverted — never editing production code), AC7 docs/skill/plan updated and .claude/skills copy byte-identical. Pre-review lint+format+test-assertions gate must show zero new violations.

Global cross-agent review of S01..S02: every AC (AC1-AC7) verified end-to-end; scope integrity (git diff origin/main -- orch/ dashboard/ executor/ scripts/ is empty — no residual production-code injection, no ai-dev/active/I-NNNNN/** created); skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md canonical QV-gate list NOT modified (no new gate — scope creep check); KNOWN_LEAK entries independently verified (each has a TODO(file-incident) placeholder + rationale, is xfail-ed, not silently skipped, listed for operator follow-up — a real Incident ID is CRITICAL); TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md changelog counts match the S01 report. Runs the full unit + integration suites — make test-integration executes the new isolation matrix; a failure there is CRITICAL.
QV: Assertion scanner (forbid new vacuous tests)
QV: Integration tests — runs the new cross-project isolation matrix

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Note the KNOWN_LEAK allowlist size, whether S09 (integration-tests) needed fix cycles from latent isolation failures, whether the second_project fixture introduced any order-dependency issues under pytest-randomly, and any high-priority Incidents filed for genuine cross-project leaks.
